summary: Cantrill expresses excitement about the Nexenta project, which combines Debian package management with the OpenSolaris kernel - something he had wished for but assumed was years away. Just three months after speculating about the possibilities of merging these systems, the Nexenta team released working ISOs that he immediately installed on his laptop. He demonstrates the combination's power by using DTrace to examine the Debian package management system.
key_points: The Nexenta project combines Debian package management (APT) with the OpenSolaris kernel
Cantrill had assumed such a combination would take years to develop, but it arrived in months
He successfully installed and ran Nexenta on his Acer Ferrari 3400 laptop
The combination allows using DTrace to examine Linux userland tools like package management
money_quotes: To be honest, when I wrote that I assumed that such a Xanadu would be a couple of years off at least – combining these systems is non-trivial.
Of course, hearing about a new technology is one thing; seeing it is quite another – I wanted to get my hands on actual bits before I got too excited.
Oh be still, my beating heart!
key_insight: The open source community can achieve ambitious technical integrations far faster than even optimistic insiders expect when motivated developers tackle the problem directly.
